title: quarantine
description: Published when a chunk or row fails validation at any stage of the pipeline. Producers: streaming-consumer (source-shape, normalization, canonical-shape, FK failures), receivers (preflight failures). Consumed by quarantine-drainer, which persists to the quarantine schema in Cloud SQL for tenant and ops viewing via dis-api.

trace_id.description: Trace_id of the chunk being quarantined. Same as the original ingress.ready / ingress.resubmit trace_id.

store_id.description: Optional. Present for row-level failures and chunk-level failures where store is known. Absent if the failure occurred before store could be resolved.

failure_stage.description: Which pipeline stage produced the failure. Preflight is receiver-side; the rest are streaming-consumer stages.

failure_type.description: Category of failure. Used for ops dashboards and tenant-facing display.

failure_reason.description: Human-readable failure description. Tenant-facing text from the validation library when applicable.

granularity.description: chunk: the whole chunk failed and no rows were processed. row: this is one of N failed rows from the chunk; other rows may have succeeded or also been quarantined.

row_index.description: For granularity=row, the index of the failed row within the chunk (0-based). Absent for granularity=chunk.

row_hash.description: For granularity=row, a content hash of the failed row. Used by the drainer for idempotent inserts. Absent for granularity=chunk.

bronze_ref.description: Foreign key to the bronze row of the chunk.

gcs_uri.description: GCS URI of the original payload. Same as the ingress event's gcs_uri.

mapping_version_id.description: Mapping version that produced this failure (post-mapping stages only). Absent for preflight and source_shape failures.

suite_ref.description: Pointer to the Pandera suite that produced the failure, when applicable. Format: 'tenant/source/version/suite_name'. Used by dis-api quarantine handler to link to suite documentation.

failure_context.description: Stage-specific details. For Pandera failures: failing column, expected vs actual, sample bad values. For FK failures: which FK, what value. Open structure; consumers project what they need.

parent_trace_id.description: If the chunk being quarantined was itself a resubmit, the original trace_id. Carried through for lineage display.
